title: The next evolution of the Agents SDK
optimized_headline: Discover What's Next for the Agents SDK: Key Innovations Ahead
raw_excerpt: OpenAI updates the Agents SDK with native sandbox execution and a model-native harness, helping developers build secure, long-running agents across files and tools.
raw_body: OpenAI updates the Agents SDK with native sandbox execution and a model-native harness, helping developers build secure, long-running agents across files and tools.
speedrun: OpenAI has updated its Agents SDK, introducing native sandbox execution and a model-native harness. This allows developers to create secure, long-running agents that can interact with various files and tools. The enhancement aims to improve the reliability and safety of these agents in real-world applications. This matters now as it could lead to more robust AI solutions that handle complex tasks more effectively.
why_it_matters: Developers can now build more secure AI agents, reducing risks associated with running untrusted code. This enhances trust in AI applications.
The update signals a shift towards more integrated and efficient AI tools, reflecting the industry's move to create safer and more adaptable AI systems.

lenses.eli12: OpenAI's latest SDK update makes it easier for developers to create AI agents that can work safely over long periods. Think of it like giving these agents a secure workspace where they can do their jobs without interruptions. This matters because it means we could see more reliable AI that handles tasks better in everyday life.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, this update means they can now build AI products that are safer and more efficient. The improved SDK can help meet user needs for reliability while potentially reducing costs associated with errors and security issues. This could lead to faster product development cycles and better user experiences.
lenses.engineer: From a technical perspective, the new native sandbox execution allows agents to run securely, minimizing risks when executing code across different environments. The model-native harness enhances compatibility and performance, which could lead to improved efficiency in task execution. This is important for developers aiming to create scalable and safe AI solutions.
